main_ingredients: bitter gourd stuffed with fish paste
allergens: gluten (wheat in some fish paste formulations)

serving.description: 1 bowl with 8 pieces of Yong Tau Foo and laksa broth
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving consists of approximately 550 g, comprising 8 pieces of assorted Yong Tau Foo items (stuffed tofu, bitter gourd, eggplant, fish balls, tofu puffs) served in a bowl of laksa broth with rice vermicelli, bean sprouts, and coconut-milk-based laksa soup.

nutrient_source.title: Yong Tau Foo (mixed items, boiled) and Laksa — HPB Singapore Foodo / FCID composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) — Foodo / Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB Foodo entry covers 8-piece Yong Tau Foo with Laksa as a combined dish. Values are derived by compositing HPB Foodo entries for: (1) Yong Tau Foo — mixed items boiled (HPB), (2) Laksa with coconut milk broth (HPB), and (3) rice vermicelli (HPB). Per-100g values represent the blended dish at the stated 550 g serving size. IMR Malaysia MyFCD and FAO/INFOODS SEA data used as cross-reference for coconut-milk broth and fish paste components. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation.

gi_source.title: Rice vermicelli GI and Laksa composite GI estimate — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 8-piece Yong Tau Foo with Laksa as a combined dish. GI estimated at 52 (low) based on: (1) rice vermicelli GI ≈ 58 (University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2008, Am J Clin Nutr); (2) protein and fat contributions from fish paste items and coconut milk broth substantially lower the overall meal GI via macronutrient interaction effect (Brand-Miller et al., peer-reviewed); (3) Laksa broth with coconut milk fat further attenuates glycaemic response. Composite meal GI estimated at 52. Glycemic load per 550 g serving calculated as: (52 × 46.75 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 24.3, rounded to 24.2.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from laksa broth and fish paste
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 420 mg sodium per 100 g (≈2310 mg per 550 g serving), this dish provides more than the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive individuals in a single meal; patients with hypertension should request reduced-broth portions, avoid drinking the soup, and limit frequency of consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycaemic load from rice vermicelli carbohydrates, partially offset by protein and fat
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 52 and glycaemic load of approximately 24 per serving, this dish poses a moderate glycaemic challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; portion control — particularly reducing the rice vermicelli quantity — and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut milk and elevated sodium from broth
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The coconut-milk-based laksa broth contributes approximately 2.9 g saturated fat per 100 g, and the high sodium load raises cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size, avoid consuming the full broth, and choose this dish only occasionally as part of a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Yong Tau Foo with Laksa (8 pieces) is a popular Singaporean hawker dish combining two beloved local food traditions: Yong Tau Foo (酿豆腐), a Hakka Chinese dish of tofu and vegetables stuffed with seasoned fish paste, and Laksa, a spicy coconut-milk noodle soup of Peranakan origin. In this dish, eight assorted Yong Tau Foo items — typically including stuffed bitter gourd, stuffed eggplant, stuffed tofu, fried tofu puffs, and fish balls — are served in a rich, aromatic laksa broth made from coconut milk, laksa paste (blended lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, dried shrimp, and chilli), and fish stock, accompanied by rice vermicelli and fresh bean sprouts. The dish is finished with a dollop of sambal chilli and a garnish of laksa leaves (Vietnamese coriander). The flavour profile is bold, spicy, and creamy, with the fish paste items providing a savoury, springy contrast to the silky broth. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-energy, protein-rich meal (approximately 98 kcal and 6.2 g protein per 100 g) with notable saturated fat from coconut milk and high sodium from the broth and fish paste. The estimated glycaemic index of 52 places it in the low-GI category, though the glycaemic load per serving is moderate at approximately 24 due to the rice vermicelli content. Clinically, the dish is of concern for hypertensive individuals due to its high sodium content, and for those with cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat from coconut milk. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, portion control of the vermicelli component is advisable. The dish is widely available at Singaporean hawker centres, food courts, and specialist Yong Tau Foo stalls, and is a staple of the local food culture.

confidence_reason: No single approved source entry covers 8-piece Yong Tau Foo with Laksa as a combined dish. Values are composited from HPB Singapore Foodo entries for Yong Tau Foo (mixed boiled items), Laksa (coconut milk broth), and rice vermicelli, cross-referenced with IMR Malaysia MyFCD and FAO/INFOODS SEA. GI is an analogous estimate based on rice vermicelli GI from the University of Sydney GI Database with macronutrient interaction adjustment. Medium confidence is appropriate for a composite estimation from approved primary regional sources.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from HPB Singapore Foodo (primary), IMR Malaysia MyFCD, and FAO/INFOODS SEA for individual components; no single approved source entry covers this exact combined dish. 2. Serving size of 550 g is an estimate for a standard hawker-centre bowl with 8 Yong Tau Foo items, laksa broth, and rice vermicelli; actual restaurant portions may vary. 3. Sodium content is particularly variable depending on broth concentration and whether the customer consumes the full soup; the value of 420 mg/100 g represents a typical full-bowl estimate. 4. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this combined dish was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of the access date. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA source data for this dish category. 6. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA source data for this dish category. 7. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA source data for this dish category. 8. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in fish paste and coconut milk components per HPB and FAO/INFOODS SEA data. 9. Halal status is typical for hawker-centre Yong Tau Foo Laksa but may vary by vendor; consumers should verify with individual restaurants. 10. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Grains and staples' (input) to 'Mixed' as this is a composite dish with significant protein, fat, and vegetable components alongside the noodle base.
